metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Each child must name the eval spec file path, fixture format (recall cases + no-fire cases), and numeric pass threshold for its overlay's eval, consistent with the rebar eval harness's load_eval_spec() contract.
findings.finding: Parent AC2 ('each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') is only partially covered: no child specifies the eval fixture format, target file path, required case count, or numeric pass threshold, making the criterion unverifiable as stated.
findings.location: child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5, child 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24, child 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3, child a010-aec8-c088-4314, child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119, child f894-ebd4-e841-404f
findings.suggested_fix: In each child's Files section, add the concrete eval spec path (e.g., src/rebar/llm/evals/specs/code-review-tests.yaml) and add an AC that names a minimum fixture count (e.g., ≥3 recall + ≥2 no-fire) and a pass threshold (e.g., recall agreement ≥ 1.0, no-fire agreement ≥ 1.0).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an instruction-locality guard to the llm-prompts overlay spec clarifying how the overlay should handle diffs that modify overlay/reviewer files themselves (including its own file), to prevent pink-elephant self-review loops.
findings.finding: The llm-prompts overlay is tasked with reviewing 'prompt, contract, and output-schema changes to LLM surfaces' but the plan provides no instruction-locality guard for the self-referential case: when the overlay reviews its own enrichment diff (or a sibling overlay's diff), it may fire on the very DSO content being added by this plan, or suppress findings it should surface. No 'do not review your own overlay file' or equivalent exception is specified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the eval mechanism for each overlay (e.g., a specific script, CI job, or deterministic rubric command) and state what 'passing' means (e.g., all recall cases fire, no VALID-pattern fires a flag) so AC2 is in-session verifiable.
findings.finding: AC2 specifies 'a passing recall + no-fire eval' but provides no eval mechanism, invocation command, or passing threshold. An executing agent cannot deterministically verify this criterion in-session without knowing what tool/script/rubric runs the eval or what score constitutes 'passing'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Verified by running `scripts/eval-overlay.sh <overlay>` (or equivalent); passing = all seeded recall cases fire AND no VALID-pattern cases fire a flag. Result logged as overlay-name.eval.json in the PR artifacts.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and link (or inline) the source for 'dso's 8 red-team concerns' and '8 AI-advantaged concerns' so an executor can populate security and performance overlays without external clarification.
findings.finding: The What section references 'dso's 8 red-team concerns' (security) and '8 AI-advantaged concerns' (performance) without identifying where these enumerations live. If the source document is not an already-accessible repo artifact, an executor cannot populate the overlays without stopping to locate or reconstruct the list.
findings.location: What — security bullet ('dso's 8 red-team concerns') and performance bullet ('8 AI-advantaged concerns')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to each bullet a file path or anchor, e.g. '(see docs/dso-security-concerns.md §Red-Team)' and '(see docs/dso-performance-concerns.md §AI-Advantaged)', or enumerate the lists directly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Scope (or a new 'Eval Artifacts' subsection) whether the recall+no-fire eval for each overlay is an inline section within the .md file, a sibling file, or a CI harness run — so that AC2 has an unambiguous artifact to gate on.
findings.finding: The eval artifacts described in What ('Each task lands with its overlay's eval (recall + no-fire)') are not enumerated in Scope, which lists only the six overlay .md files. AC2 then gates on 'a passing recall + no-fire eval' without the plan body clarifying whether these evals are separate files, inline sections, or ephemeral runs — creating a cross-section gap between What's deliverables and Scope's file list, and leaving AC2 potentially unmeasurable.
findings.location: What section ('Each task lands with its overlay's eval') vs Scope section (6 .md files) vs AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to Scope such as: 'Each overlay .md file includes an embedded ## Eval section containing the recall + no-fire test cases; no separate eval file is produced.' Or, if evals are separate, add their file paths to Scope (e.g. code-review-tests.eval.md).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the 'both-halves' overlay contract (recall + FP-discrimination, Pass-2 stays domain-blind) in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or named design doc, and reference it from the plan.
findings.finding: The plan introduces and references a 'locked principle' (each overlay carries both recall patterns AND FP-discrimination halves) as an architectural contract governing all six overlays, but names no document — ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc — where this principle is captured or will be updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a task: 'Update CLAUDE.md (or create ADR-NNN) documenting the locked principle that every specialist overlay must carry both recall patterns and FP-guards, and that the Pass-2 verifier must remain domain-blind.' Reference the document path from the Why section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale sentence to the plan (Why or Approach section) stating that inline enrichment of each overlay's `.md` prompt text is the correct seam because it is the exclusive instruction surface the specialist LLM receives, and that the Pass-2 verifier's domain-blindness is preserved by keeping all DSO content within the overlay boundary.
findings.finding: The plan states what will be enriched (the overlay .md files) and invokes the locked principle as motivation, but does not give a positive rationale for WHY inline prompt enrichment of the overlay file is the correct mechanism — e.g. that it is the only seam the specialist LLM sees, that no injection or sibling-file mechanism exists, and that the Pass-2 verifier's domain-blindness is architecturally enforced. Without this, an implementer cannot distinguish the approach from alternatives that would require new machinery.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's Why or a new Approach section: 'The overlay .md file is the exclusive instruction surface each specialist LLM reads; enriching it inline is the only seam that reaches the model without touching the shared kernel or Pass-2 verifier. The domain-blind verifier invariant is preserved because all DSO recall patterns and FP-guards stay within the overlay boundary.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out in the tests overlay AC (or Scope) that the enriched `code-review-tests.md` prompt must encode the 'NOT-flag base rules do not apply to test-correctness' carve-out as inline prompt instruction, and add a recall eval case that would fail if the carve-out were absent.
findings.finding: The plan's 'tests' overlay enrichment includes a load-bearing behavioral distinction — 'NOT-flag base rules do not apply to test-correctness' — that must be encoded in the enriched overlay prompt text to take effect, but the plan does not call this out as an explicit authoring requirement for the tests overlay. If omitted from the prompt, the LLM will apply base-overlay suppression logic to test-correctness findings, defeating the recall half.
findings.location: Plan section: What — tests overlay bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define (inline in the plan or in a referenced canonical source) the full value vocabularies for every named DSO concept: Four-Criterion test, Rule-5 structural-artifact exception, VALID-patterns-not-to-flag, the 8 red-team concerns, rationalizations-to-reject, 8 AI-advantaged concerns, asymmetric-change/producer-consumer drift, delete+replace symmetry, and ui-artifact/domain-mismatch/contract-drift.
findings.finding: Multiple named decision-rule vocabularies central to the DSO enrichment are referenced in the plan but never defined anywhere in the codebase or plan text: 'Four-Criterion test', 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception', 'VALID-patterns-not-to-flag' (tests overlay); '8 red-team concerns', 'rationalizations-to-reject' (security overlay); '8 AI-advantaged concerns', 'bright-line reasoning' (performance overlay); 'asymmetric-change / producer-consumer drift' (api-compat); 'delete+replace symmetry' (db-migrations); 'ui-artifact + domain-mismatch + contract-drift' (llm-prompts). An executor receiving this plan cannot write any overlay content without first knowing what these concepts mean.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria' in the security overlay spec with the canonical routing-index criterion ids: 'secret-detection' (gitleaks sentinel) and 'high-critical-security' (rebar.builtin.security.* prefix).
findings.finding: The security overlay description says 'hard exclusions deferring to the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria by name', but no criterion named 'opengrep' exists in criteria_routing.json. The actual DET criterion ids are 'secret-detection' (gitleaks sentinel) and 'high-critical-security' (rebar.builtin.security.* prefix, implemented via opengrep/semgrep). Referencing 'opengrep DET criteria' by that name gives the executor an undefined identifier to defer to.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the exact criterion ids from criteria_routing.json: defer to `secret-detection` for secrets and `high-critical-security` for high/critical static-analysis hits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision rule to AC line 2 specifying: (a) the eval scoring threshold that constitutes 'passing', (b) the fixture schema or reference to it, and (c) what action is required if an overlay's eval does not meet the threshold before the task closes.
findings.finding: AC line 2 states 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' but the plan specifies no fallback or decision rule for what happens if an overlay's eval does not pass — the sub-step failure path is entirely unspecified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child 0f8f must produce a named, reusable overlay-authoring pattern (e.g., an authoring guide, fixture template, or CLAUDE.md entry) that the five dependent children explicitly reference and follow.
findings.finding: Child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5 is intended to establish the canonical 'overlay-authoring + eval pattern' that all five dependent children inherit, but neither child 0f8f nor the parent defines what that pattern consists of (no schema, fixture format, or authoring checklist), leaving the dependency semantically empty.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 0f8f's Acceptance Criteria: 'Produces a documented overlay-authoring + eval template (format, fixture schema, pass threshold) referenced by all five sibling children.' Add a corresponding Files entry for that template artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Each child that references 'dso content' must name the exact source document (file path or external URL) from which recall patterns and FP-guards are drawn, or inline the content directly.
findings.finding: Five children (d71f, a010, f894, 3cb3, 1ead) reference 'dso content' or 'dso's [N] concerns' as the source of their recall patterns, but 'dso' is never defined in any child ticket, the parent plan, or any accessible canonical document in the repo, creating an unresolvable dependency gap for every executing agent.
findings.location: child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119, child a010-aec8-c088-4314, child f894-ebd4-e841-404f, child 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3, child 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add a canonical 'dso rubric' document to the repo (e.g., docs/dso-overlay-rubric.md) and reference it in each child's Files section, or (b) inline the full recall + FP-guard lists directly in each child's What section so no external source is assumed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the parent or each sibling whether the 'base NOT-flag rules do not apply' exception is limited to the tests overlay or applies to all six overlays; add this clarification to each relevant child's AC.
findings.finding: Child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5 introduces a unique scoping rule ('Base NOT-flag rules do not apply to test-correctness findings') that has no equivalent in any sibling, creating an asymmetric and unreconciled treatment of the base overlay's NOT-flag rules across the six overlays.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a statement to the parent plan under Scope (or add an AC) that explicitly states the scope of the NOT-flag exception: e.g., 'For all six overlays, base NOT-flag rules are subordinate to overlay-specific correctness findings' — then remove the ambiguity by reflecting this in each child.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] At least one child (or a shared step in the parent plan) must specify a concrete mechanism — such as a verify_command, grep check, or CI assertion — that confirms code-review-verify.md has not been modified or gained domain-specific rubric after the six overlays land.
findings.finding: No child specifies a mechanism to prevent or detect leakage of domain-specific rubric into code-review-verify.md (parent AC3 / each child's AC3), even though all six children share this constraint against the same file, creating implicit shared-state risk with no coordination.
findings.location: child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5, child 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24, child 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3, child a010-aec8-c088-4314, child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119, child f894-ebd4-e841-404f
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 0f8f (as pattern-setter) a verify_command such as: `git diff HEAD -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md | diff /dev/null -` (assert no changes), and reference it in all sibling children or in the parent's completion criteria.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
